manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: fish, plot, bar, hook, honey, oil, cat, saturn, car.
Your opponent's team words are: lemon, organ, turkey, laser, force, berry, calf, doctor.
Distractor words are: sink, millionaire, game, tail, queen, nurse, figure.
The kill words are: club.

message.content:
CLUE: automaker
TARGETS: car, saturn, oil
message.reasoning:
We need to give a single-word clue that relates to some of the team's words: fish, plot, bar, hook, honey... etc. Must not be any of the words or contain parts of them. Also can't contain parts of opponent or distractor? It says cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them. So clue cannot contain part of any listed word. Can't have "fish" substring, "plot", "bar", "hook", "honey", "oil", "cat", "saturn", "car". Also shouldn't contain "lemon", etc. So keep clue simple.

Goal: maximize number of target words.

Potential clue: "brew". That relates to honey? Not really. Maybe "sea" relates to fish, hook, bar? Not. "write" relates to plot, bar? hmm.

Maybe "persistence"? Not.

We could target "hook, fish, bar" with "tackle"? Tackle is fishing gear includes hook and fish. Also "tackle box" includes bar? Not.

"Saturn" is a planet, also a ring. "Ring" could relate to bar? No.

"Engine" relates to car, oil. Also "oil" directly. That's two. "Engine" not in list. Does engine relate to fish? not.

Maybe "feline" relates to cat only.

We can try to get three: "bait" relates to fish, hook, maybe honey (as sweet bait). Also bar? Not. Bait is used for fish/hook. So clue "bait". That includes "bait" not in any list. It contains part "bar"? No. "bait" contains "ait" not.

So TARGETS: fish, hook, honey? honey can be used as bait. Possibly. But risk.

Alternatively "automobile" for car, oil, maybe bar? Not. "garage". Hmm.

"Saturn" and "car" both are models by "Ford"? Not. "Saturn" was brand of car. So clue "automaker". Could relate to car and Saturn (both car brands). Also maybe "oil". That's three. Good.

Clue: "automaker". Does it contain "car"? No. Contains "auto" part of "organ"? No. It's okay.

Check substring: "car" not present fully; "automaker" contains "maker" not "car". Good.

But "automaker" also includes "auto" which is part of "organ"? No. Good.

Thus relate to car, Saturn (both car makes), oil (car uses oil). That's three.

Thus output:

CLUE: automaker
TARGETS: car, saturn, oil

Make sure format exactly.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide a comma-separated list of up to 3 words from the following list that best relate or are most closely associated with the word 'automaker'. Always start your list of guess(es) with 'GUESS: ' and do not include any other text in your answer.

nurse, oil, force, plot, hook, doctor, turkey, millionaire, sink, figure, car, calf, queen, game, organ, tail, fish, laser, bar, cat, honey, berry, club, saturn, lemon

message.reasoning: We need to output "GUESS: " then up to 3 words from list that best relate to "automaker". Automaker is related to car, maybe oil, maybe force (like horsepower?), maybe figure? The list includes "car". So we definitely include car. Also "oil" is related (oil for engines). Possibly "force" (force as power), but "oil" seems more directly related. So output "GUESS: car, oil". Up to 3 words, ok
